reasoning: Based on data available just days before the FOMC meeting scheduled for October 28-29, 2025, the market consensus is overwhelmingly in favor of a 25 basis point rate cut. According to the CME FedWatch Tool on October 24, 2025, there is a 98.9% probability of a 25 bps cut. Furthermore, prominent Fed officials, including Governor Waller, have indicated their support for a 25 bps cut to support softening labor markets. The Fed is currently in its blackout period, during which officials cannot comment further, meaning futures markets are extremely unlikely to move significantly prior to the meeting. Although a tail-risk event could alter the decision at the last minute, the probability of the Fed defying a 98.9% market expectation is exceptionally low. Therefore, a 25 bps cut is near-certainty.
